mission.title: Renew expired cluster credentials when the console reports EXPIRED_CREDENTIALS
mission.description: Repair mission for expired cluster credentials. Use it when the console preflight reports EXPIRED_CREDENTIALS, when kubectl starts failing with certificate or token expiry errors, or when cloud-provider exec credentials need to be refreshed before the console can reconnect.

steps.title: Confirm the expired credential symptom
steps.description: Capture the current context and verify whether the failure is coming from an expired client certificate, an expired exec-plugin token, or an unreachable API server.
commands:
kubectl config current-context
kubectl cluster-info
kubectl auth whoami

steps.title: Inspect kubeconfig and certificate expiration dates
steps.description: Review the active kubeconfig user and inspect any embedded client certificate expiry. On kubeadm control-plane nodes, also inspect the cluster certificate inventory to see whether renewal is required.
commands:
kubectl config view --raw --minify
kubectl config view --raw --minify -o jsonpath='{.users[0].user.client-certificate-data}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -dates 2>/dev/null || echo "No embedded client certificate in current kubeconfig user"
sudo kubeadm certs check-expiration 2>/dev/null || echo "kubeadm certificate inventory unavailable on this host"

steps.title: Renew kubeadm-managed certificates when applicable
steps.description: If the cluster is kubeadm-managed and the certificates are expired or near expiry, renew them on a control-plane node and refresh the generated admin kubeconfig before using it locally again.
commands:
sudo kubeadm certs renew all
sudo kubeadm init phase kubeconfig admin
mkdir -p "$HOME/.kube" && sudo cp /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf "$HOME/.kube/config" && sudo chown "$USER" "$HOME/.kube/config" && chmod 600 "$HOME/.kube/config"

steps.title: Refresh cloud provider exec credentials
steps.description: If the kubeconfig uses a cloud exec plugin instead of embedded certificates, refresh the provider login so kubectl can mint a fresh token. Run the command that matches your cluster provider.
commands:
aws eks update-kubeconfig --name "${EKS_CLUSTER_NAME:?set EKS_CLUSTER_NAME}" --region "${AWS_REGION:?set AWS_REGION}"
gcloud container clusters get-credentials "${GKE_CLUSTER_NAME:?set GKE_CLUSTER_NAME}" --region "${GKE_REGION:?set GKE_REGION}" --project "${GCP_PROJECT:?set GCP_PROJECT}"
az aks get-credentials --resource-group "${AKS_RESOURCE_GROUP:?set AKS_RESOURCE_GROUP}" --name "${AKS_CLUSTER_NAME:?set AKS_CLUSTER_NAME}" --overwrite-existing

steps.title: Verify connectivity after renewal
steps.description: After renewing certificates or refreshing tokens, confirm the active identity works again and that the console can reuse the same kubeconfig without EXPIRED_CREDENTIALS.
commands:
kubectl auth whoami
kubectl get namespaces --request-timeout=15s
kubectl get nodes -o wide

troubleshooting.title: The kubeconfig uses an exec plugin, not client certificates
troubleshooting.description: If the openssl check prints no certificate data, inspect the kubeconfig for an exec stanza and refresh the cloud login or SSO session instead of trying kubeadm certificate renewal.

troubleshooting.title: kubeadm commands are unavailable
troubleshooting.description: Run kubeadm renewal only on a kubeadm control-plane node. Managed services such as EKS, GKE, and AKS rotate control-plane certificates for you, so refresh the local kubeconfig or provider login instead.

troubleshooting.title: Credentials were renewed but kubectl still fails
troubleshooting.description: Confirm KUBECONFIG points at the renewed file, then rerun kubectl config current-context and kubectl config view --raw --minify to make sure the console is reading the updated credentials.

resolution.summary: Expired certificates or cloud-provider tokens were renewed and cluster access was revalidated, resolving EXPIRED_CREDENTIALS for kubectl and the console.

prerequisites.resources: A working kubeconfig context and, for kubeadm renewal, sudo access to a control-plane node.
prerequisites.description: Set the appropriate cloud environment variables before running the provider-specific refresh command.
